interviews.title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: it’s ironic that my business partner and my best friend, Stanley, was probably with Buddy in Binghamton when I was eleven.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jean Bach
about_quotes.evidence: Stanley Kay, the drummer with Shorty Sherock’s orchestra... Stanley Kay is the founding father of that band, and he manages them
about_quotes.doc_title: Jean Bach interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: John LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: And when I had friends that were managers, like Stanley Kay, he would go to bat for me, he’d find the money somewhere.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: When he called me – it was actually his manager, Stanley Kay, called me about it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Anat Cohen
about_quotes.evidence: And Stanley Kay, the manager of Diva asked me, “Where is your clarinet?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Anat Cohen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2021
